current_hypothesis: The AI content creation stack has matured to the point where a solo creator or small team can produce broadcast-quality multimedia at 95%+ cost savings. The field has bifurcated into cloud SaaS and local open-source stacks. Native audio is now table stakes in video. Legal risk (copyright, voice cloning, AI music) is the primary non-technical blocker for commercial adoption.
open_questions: How will UMG v. Suno (summer 2026) reshape AI music licensing?
